title: Extra-Wide Media Storage Wall
summary: A wide two-door media cabinet with a fixed middle partition and adjustable shelf-pin shelving behind each door — a plywood carcass with 1x trim and full-overlay doors that hide the center divider.
description:
This storage wall's doors are the clever part: each door is sized wider than its own compartment specifically to cover the middle partition, so the finished piece reads as one continuous cabinet front with no visible center divider — get the door widths right and the partition genuinely disappears. Build the plywood carcass first (bottom, middle partitions, sides, top, back) before touching the face trim; the middle partitions' exact position is what the door widths are measured against, so that placement has to be right before anything else is cut to fit it.

Trim goes on in a specific order for a reason: top trim first (fastened to the partitions and sides, since you can't reach into the top from outside once the case is closed), then a top EDGE trim piece fastened through its face into the top of the case — that second trim piece exists purely because the first one can't reach the top surface itself.

Build each door as its own frame-and-panel assembly — rails and stiles glued to a plywood back panel, clamped flat with weight on top while curing — then hang with either butterfly hinges or European face-frame hinges. **Shelves are adjustable on shelf pins**, drilled 1" in from the front and back at 1/2" depth with a 1/4" bit — a genuinely useful detail for a media cabinet whose contents (game consoles, boxes, speakers) tend to vary in height more than a bookshelf's.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.part: Top trim and top edge trim

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Fasten the bottom to the middle partitions with 2" fasteners. Fasten the sides to the bottom, leaving a 1-3/4" clearance under the bottom shelf. Fasten the top to the sides and middle partitions, keeping the partitions at their measured positions.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Fasten the back panel with 1-1/4" fasteners to the top, bottom, sides, and middle partition.

steps.title: Add the trim
steps.body: Fasten the top trim to the middle partitions and sides. Fasten the top edge trim through its face into the top of the case. Fasten the side trim to the base unit, keeping outside edges flush. Fasten the bottom trim last.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Fasten the rails to the back of each door panel with glue and clamps, then fasten the stiles the same way. Lay flat to dry with weight on top. Edge-band the top and inside edges if staining.

steps.title: Hang the doors and add moulding
steps.body: Test-fit each door in its opening, then attach with hinges. Add optional base moulding to dress up the bottom.

steps.title: Add adjustable shelves
steps.body: Drill shelf-pin holes 1" from the front and back on both middle partitions, 1/2" deep, with a 1/4" bit. Insert shelf pins and place the shelves. Edge-band the front edges if staining.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces, add pulls or knobs, and finish with primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Wide two-door media storage cabinet with a hidden center divider
